meaning: vi.<美俚>做细小而无用的事n.细小而无用的事,手工品
examples: It's also likely that the whole thing will be a boondoggle.
He spent too much time on boondoggle, and that's why he went nowhere.
An investigative reporter discovered that one of the projects we funded was a boondoggle.
Penney stake and regime change have been a more pathetic boondoggle than any Herbalife distributorship.
Far too much money has been spent on boondoggle projects of one sort or another.
